rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER DAVE O'DONNELL, PATRICIA GILLESPIE AMENDED MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD is GRANTED. No opposition on the merits on file. Prevailing party to prepare an order and judgment and submit the documents for execution on January 16, 2025 unless the parties reach a resolution/payment is made as referenced in the response. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TEED VS. CLAY STREET LLC (A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants' Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action (Lis Pendens) And For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2024 line 3. rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER ET AL VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Iso
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2024 line 7. DEDEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES ISO is GRANTED without leave to amend. The unlawful detainer complaint is based on a valid notice terminating the tenancy. The operative notice attached to the complaint is based expressly on "breach of the terms of your rental or lease agreement as follows: failure to sign the lease." A lease dated April 2, 2014 is attached to the complaint and contains no terms requiring the tenant to sign a lease. The operative notice mentions no other just causes for eviction under SFRO. Complaint alleges that this tenancy is subject to SFRO. SFRO requires that the landlord inform the tenant is writing on or before the date upon which notice to vacate is given of the grounds under which possession is sought. In the late-filed opposition Plaintiffs claim that they are entitled to possession under SFRO 37.9(a)(5). This "just cause" for eviction is not listed as the basis for termination of tenancy, only breach of the lease, is listed. Additionally, Plaintiff fails to show how the 4-page proposed lease is materially the same and does not contradict SFRO. For example, (this is a non-exhaustive lease) see paragraphs 11, 24 and 25 of the proposed lease attached to the Complaint and compare it to the existing lease (attached to the Complaint) and SFRO. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER ET AL VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF SAAM BARRAGER, BETTY TROMBATORE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER is DENIED. Defendant's motion, per the Notice of Motion is "made pursuant to the provisions of Code of Civil procedure Section 2030 and is based on the fact that Attorney for Defendant and Defendant lied to the Court to effect for themselves a favorable outcome." The motion is further based, per the opening sentence of the Memorandum of Points and Authorities on the basis that "Plaintiff timely requested possession of their property by default, but the clerk incorrectly rejected the default paperwork." None of the above are grounds for the Motion for Summary Judgment governed by CCP 437c. The Court notes that Plaintiff's attempt to secure a default by submitting a Request for Default at 8:48 a.m. on November 5, 2024 while Defendant has an active demurrer on calendar was premature. The Court did not make a final ruling taking said demurrer off calendar until after 9:30 a.m. on November 5, 2024. By the time the Court's ruling was final, the Defendant submitted an Answer for filing at 9:21 a.m. Defendant had an active demurrer on file between September 20, 2024 and 9:30 a.m. on November 5, 2024. Defendant filed an Answer at 9:21 a.m. on November 5, 2024. At not time was the defendant in default and was actively participating in this case by making a general appearance. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
